# Tessera: M3 Results

**Status:** first full sweep, 200 seeds/config, deterministic. Generated by
`sweep.py` (RQ1, RQ3a, RQ3b); raw JSON in `results/`. Companion to
EXPERIMENTS.md; every number here is regenerable with
`TESSERA_SEEDS=200 python sweep.py all`.

These are prototype-simulator results under a *perfect global sequencer*
(EXPERIMENTS §8), so every liveness number is an **upper bound** on what a
real ordering layer delivers. Read them as "the protocol logic itself does
not fail here," not "this is deployable at these rates."

## Headline: the M3 go/no-go gate is GO

RQ3 was the gate: find the liveness cliff, and if realistic conditions sit
past it, repurpose the project as a negative result. They do not. Two
findings decide it:

1. **Steady packet loss does not threaten liveness — at all — up to 20%**
   (RQ3a). Rung-1 retransmit absorbs every gap, because with N peers each
   holding the last W ciphertexts, a laggard almost always finds a server.
   Zero rejoins, zero deaths, across i.i.d. and bursty loss.
2. **The real limit is outage-vs-window, and the swarm survives crossing
   it** (RQ3b). When a correlated wave of agents is unreachable for longer
   than the window's *time span*, they can no longer Rung-1 recover and
   rejoin (Rung 2) — in a mild storm — but the swarm never dies and stays
   consistent (0% dead, forks/continuity-breaks 0 throughout).

The design consequence is a single rule, and the spec already has the
mechanism for it: **size the window's time-floor above the expected
correlated-outage duration** (PROTOCOL §12's "64 msgs *or* 30 s" — the
*or* is load-bearing).

## RQ3a — Liveness under steady loss

N=15, rate 0.2 msg/s/agent, window 64, 200 seeds. Both i.i.d. and
Gilbert–Elliott burst loss (mean burst 10), loss from 0.1% to 20%:

| loss | Rung-1-only | mean rejoins | swarm dead | ended behind | forks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0.1% → 20% (i.i.d.)  | 1.000 | 0.00 | 0.000 | 0.000 | 0 |
| 0.1% → 20% (burst)   | 1.000 | 0.00 | 0.000 | 0.000 | 0 |

Every cell is identical: 100% of loss is absorbed at Rung 1, no rejoins,
no deaths, no forks — even at 20% loss with bursts. **H3b (a loss-driven
rejoin-storm cliff) is not supported.** The dangerous variable is not loss
rate.

## RQ3b — Liveness under correlated churn (the cliff)

N=15, 40% of agents (6 of 15) go offline together for duration D; window W
varied. The cliff tracks the window's *time span* `W / (N·rate)`:

| W | W secs | outage D | crosses W? | any-rejoin | mean rejoins | max | dead | behind |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 32 | 10.7 | 5   | no  | 0.000 | 0.00 | 0  | 0.000 | 0.000 |
| 32 | 10.7 | 15  | yes | 0.185 | 0.86 | 7  | 0.000 | 0.005 |
| 32 | 10.7 | 25  | yes | 0.970 | 5.88 | 8  | 0.000 | 0.010 |
| 64 | 21.3 | 15  | no  | 0.000 | 0.00 | 0  | 0.000 | 0.000 |
| 64 | 21.3 | 25  | yes | 0.000 | 0.00 | 0  | 0.000 | 0.000 |
| 64 | 21.3 | 40  | yes | 0.730 | 4.34 | 9  | 0.000 | 0.030 |
| 64 | 21.3 | 60  | yes | 1.000 | 6.18 | 11 | 0.000 | 0.040 |
| 128 | 42.7 | 40 | no  | 0.000 | 0.00 | 0  | 0.000 | 0.000 |
| 128 | 42.7 | 60 | yes | 0.025 | 0.10 | 6  | 0.000 | 0.000 |
| 128 | 42.7 | 100| yes | 1.000 | 6.17 | 11 | 0.000 | 0.045 |

Reading it:

- **The cliff is sharp and sits at D ≈ W-seconds.** W=64 (21.3 s): zero
  rejoins at D=25, then 0.73 at D=40, then 1.00 at D=60 — the "always
  recovers → always rejoins" transition spans roughly a 2× change in
  outage. Tripling W (32→128) triples the safe outage (≈11 s → ≈43 s),
  confirming the ratio, not the absolutes, is what matters.
- **There is a rejoin storm, and it is bounded.** Past the cliff, mean
  rejoins is ~6 — essentially all 6 offline agents — and *max* reaches
  11–12, i.e. some rejoins strand other laggards mid-recovery and force
  further rejoins (H3b's cascade, but churn-triggered, not loss-triggered).
  It amplifies ~2× at worst; it does not run away.
- **The swarm survives the cliff.** `dead` is 0.000 everywhere and
  `ended behind` peaks at 6.5%; the cost of crossing the cliff is
  epoch-change churn, not collapse. That is the difference between a cost
  cliff (tune W) and a correctness cliff (redesign).

## RQ1 — Detection

N=5, rate 0.5 msg/s/agent, adversary injected at t=100, 200 seeds. Latency
is measured from injection; positions are chain slots elapsed.

| adversary | det. rate | median latency | median pos | attrib ok | false pos | forged acc |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A3 speaking clone   | 0.955 | 0.60 s | 1 | 1.00 | 0.000 | 0 |
| A3 stale-restore    | 0.990 | (speaks after 3 s delay) | — | 1.00 | 0.000 | 0 |
| A3 silent clone     | 0.000 | — | — | — | 0.000 | 0 |
| A4 equivocator      | 1.000 | 2.02 s | 0–1 | 0.93 | 0.000 | 0 |
| A6 impostor         | 0.000 | — | — | — | 0.000 | 0 |
| A1 eavesdropper     | 0.000 | — | — | — | 0.000 | 0 |

Reading it:

- **The positives hold across seeds, not just one lucky run.** A speaking
  clone is caught in a median of 1 chain position with perfect
  attribution; an equivocator is *always* detected.
- **The negatives hold too, and one is a finding.** A6 and A1 produce no
  detections and `forged_accepted` is 0 everywhere — an impostor cannot
  speak. The silent clone (A3-silent) is *never* detected: a clone that
  does not speak is invisible to the transcript layer (this corrected
  PROTOCOL §8; see EXPERIMENTS H1a′).
- **Attribution ≠ detection for equivocation.** A4 is detected 100% of the
  time but the *first alarm* names the true equivocator only 93% of the
  time. Most correct hits come from the equivocator's own heartbeat
  diverging on the far side of the fork (`ChainDivergence`); the wrong 7%
  are `CloneEvidence` events that finger the first *innocent* member to
  message across the fork. Definitive attribution needs the two
  contradictory signed wires — which always exist — not the real-time
  event. PROTOCOL §8's "the signed headers identify the author" is true
  only once both headers are in hand.

## What these results change

- EXPERIMENTS H3b is revised: the liveness cliff is **churn-driven, not
  loss-driven**, and is a survivable cost cliff, not a death cliff.
- The window's **time-floor** is promoted from a detail to the primary
  liveness tuning knob; the design rule is `W_seconds > expected
  correlated-outage`.
- Equivocation **attribution** gets an honest caveat (first-alarm vs.
  evidence-in-hand), added to the §8 discussion.

## Honest limits of this measurement

- Perfect sequencer → liveness numbers are upper bounds (EXPERIMENTS §8).
- One traffic shape (Poisson) and one swarm size band; the real-trace and
  N∈{25,100} sweeps from EXPERIMENTS §1/§3 are not yet run.
- Prototype crypto (HMAC-as-KDF, sealed-box-not-HPKE); irrelevant to
  message-count/latency results, relevant to any CPU claim (none made
  here).
